item.title: Could Australia win Eurovision? The odds are looking Goodrem
item.description: Delta Goodrem has progressed to the grand final of the Eurovision Song Contest, where she is one of the favourites to win.

item.title: How AI is rewriting resumes and the ASX ends tough week lower
item.description: On this episode of the SBS On the Money podcast, Stephanie Youssef speaks with Michael Page Senior Managing Director David George about the growing use of artificial intelligence in recruitment, with more job seekers and employers turning to AI to write resumes, job ads and interview questions. Plus, Ricardo Gonçalves is joined by Tribeca Investment Partners Portfolio Manager Todd Warren to unpack a volatile week on the Australian sharemarket, as profit taking in the mining sector helped drag the ASX 1.3% lower for the week.

item.title: INTERVIEW: Angus Taylor questioned by Chief Political Correspondent Anna Henderson
item.description: Opposition leader Angus Taylor says a Coalition government would remove 75,000 visa overstayers from Australia. In his budget reply, he also announced that non-citizens will be blocked from accessing 17 welfare programs. One Nation says Angus Taylor has copied its migration policy. SBS Chief Political Correspondent Anna Henderson asked Mr Taylor whether he's moving further to the right to try to court One Nation voters.

item.title: Hopes for 'lasting peace': Israel and Lebanon extend ceasefire by 45-days
item.description: A US official has described the talks between the countries as "highly productive".

item.title: How Australian housing became a generational budget battleground
item.description: Housing taxation changes have upset some investors. If the government can win the support of young people, that may not matter.

item.title: Next-generation Indigenous women are leading the way on employment growth
item.description: A new study has shown that employment rates for Indigenous men and women are now nearly equal.

item.title: Australia has a growing $56 billion problem - is this a solution?
item.description: As more women face mental health challenges, experts are pushing for more single-gender psychiatric wards.

item.title: Government to reassess fuel excise extension as reserves swell, July cutoff nears
item.description: Australia remains at level two of a fuel plan that asks users only buy what they need and take voluntary steps to use less.

item.title: INTERVIEW: Greens leader Larissa Waters responds to the Federal Budget
item.description: Greens leader Larissa Waters says the federal government should be setting out to build more public and affordable housing similar to the way previous governments used to build large-scale public housing estates. Senator Waters says housing affordability demands bold reform but says the federal government changes to property taxes in the Budget amounts to tinkering around the edges. SBS Chief Political Correspondent Anna Henderson asked her whether the Greens will back the changes to negative gearing and capital gains.

item.title: Israel’s Eurovision voting campaign is in the spotlight — again. Here’s why
item.description: Israel's public broadcaster was formally warned by Eurovision last week

item.title: Gaza mosques announce Hamas military leader's death
item.description: Witnesses said mosques ⁠announced ‌Izz al-Din al-Haddad's "martyrdom" after Israel's military said ‌airstrikes had ‌targeted ⁠him.

item.title: Prepper used to be a dirty word. But these 'ordinary' people think it's just sensible now
item.description: One lives in the bush for $50 a week. Another grows vegetables in South Australia. Meet Australia's doomsday preppers.

item.title: Antisemitism, Zionism and Israel: Royal commission hearings take a political turn
item.description: Differentiating antisemitism and criticism of Israel was one focal point of the commission's second week of hearings.

item.title: Tanya was struggling to cope with daily tasks. When she found out why, it was life-changing
item.description: One in four women in Australia faces mental health challenges. However, some are at risk when seeking help in mixed-gender hospital wards. An Australian psychiatrist is working to change that.

item.title: The Aussies who could be caught in the middle of the housing tax shake-up
item.description: Who are the Australians who could be caught in the middle of housing tax reforms in the federal budget? The government says changes to negative gearing and capital gains tax are designed to improve housing affordability and level the playing field for younger generations. But some experts warn the reforms could also create unintended consequences for Australians trying to build wealth and break into the property market.

item.title: Eurovision 2026: Bulgaria sweeps grand final as Australia's Delta Goodrem places fourth
item.description: The finals weren't without controversy, boos erupting from the audience as Israel's points totals were announced.

item.title: Who is DARA, the Bulgarian singer who won Eurovision 2026?
item.description: DARA's song Bangaranga swept voting at the famed song competition, beating out Israel, Romania and Australia.

item.title: Coalition courts One Nation voters with hardline migration plan | Midday News Bulletin 17 May 2026
item.description: Angus Taylor defends the Coalition's immigration policies amid heavy criticism; A Canadian who sailed on a hantavirus-hit cruise ship tests positive for the disease; and in football, Sam Kerr scores a winning goal for Chelsea in her last game for the club.

item.title: A major tax deadline is coming next year — could it spark a rush to sell?
item.description: The changes to CGT will take effect starting in July 2027, giving investors time to decide what to do with their properties.

item.title: WHO declares Ebola outbreak an international health emergency
item.description: The outbreak of the highly contagious disease began in the Democratic Republic of the Congo and Uganda.

item.title: EV battery supply chains in focus as China halts sulphuric acid exports
item.description: China ceasing exports of sulphuric acid is expected to put pressure on both EV battery and fertiliser supply chains

item.title: One Nation's popularity is rising. Which Australians are supporting the party and why?
item.description: Australians from across the political spectrum give their views on One Nation following the party's recent by-election victory.

item.title: Bigger than ever: What makes this year's FIFA World Cup unique
item.description: The 2026 FIFA World Cup will span three countries, feature 104 matches and introduce new measures to curb time-wasting.

item.title: 'Huge step forward': Ozempic endorsed to treat obesity and heart disease in Australian first
item.description: Two-thirds of Australians are at risk of cardiovasular disease. Could GLP-1 medications be the answer?

item.title: 'Huge step forward': Ozempic endorsed to treat obesity and heart disease in Australian first
item.description: Two-thirds of Australians are at risk of cardiovasular disease. Could GLP-1 medications be the answer?

item.title: This charity receives millions from the government. Workers say conditions were 'like a jail'
item.description: Migrant workers contracted under the PALM scheme to labour hire company Madec Australia say they are being treated unfairly.

item.title: Ukrainian drone strikes kill four in Russia in its biggest attack in over a year
item.description: Volodymyr Zelenskyy says Ukraine's increased attacks on Moscow have produced a "change in the balance of actions at the front".

item.title: What is Ebola and what makes the latest outbreak so concerning?
item.description: The WHO has declared an international health emergency in multiple African countries over the spread of the deadly disease.

item.title: Australia works with international countries to co-ordinate response to Ebola | SBS News in Easy English 18 May 2026
item.description: A daily 5 minute news wrap for English learners

item.title: Jim Chalmers defends budget tax reforms following critical polls
item.description: A Resolve poll suggested Angus Taylor had overtaken Anthony Albanese as preferred prime minister for the first time.

item.title: Labor defends budget housing tax changes despite critical polls
item.description: The contest continues over the future of Australia's tax system following last week's Federal Budget. The Coalition has flagged its own bold policies on tax and housing, while the Treasurer is working to sell a trio of changes affecting negative gearing, capital gains and trusts. Two new opinion polls suggest his federal budget has been poorly received by voters - but Jim Chalmers has declared the long-term gains from controversial housing tax changes are worth the short-term political pain.

item.title: WHO declares global health emergency in Congo, Uganda outbreak of Ebola
item.description: The World Health Organisation has declared an Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of Congo and Uganda is a public health emergency of international concern. The WHO says it does not meet the criteria of a pandemic emergency, but that countries sharing land borders with the DRC are at high risk of further spread.

item.title: ASX slumps to seven week slow as global inflation fears escalate
item.description: The ASX200 has fallen to a seven-week low as surging oil prices and rising inflation fears rattle global markets. In this episode of SBS On the Money, Ricardo Gonçalves speaks with David Scutt from StoneX Group Inc. about the market sell-off, the impact of the Middle East crisis on energy prices, and why investors are now bracing for possible interest rate rises in the United States after inflation climbed to a three-year high. The episode also looks at sharp losses for Brambles and major miners including BHP.

item.title: Rainy days ahead for much of eastern Australia, as a dry winter looms
item.description: It's going to be a wet couple of days for Australians on the east coast, while the west will be dry and settled.

item.title: No gloves, no approval: Inside the fight club bringing bare knuckle boxing to backyards
item.description: For these men, the desire to fight in bare knuckle backyard bouts outweighs the risk of injury or even imprisonment.

item.title: Cuba warns of 'bloodbath' amid Trump pressure: Is a US attack looming?
item.description: Relations between the two countries have deteriorated sharply in recent days. Experts say Trump's aims in the region are unclear.

item.title: US holds off planned Iran attack as nations resume talks
item.description: United States President Donald Trump says he has held off on an attack on Iran that was planned for today after Gulf countries' request. But President Trump says the U-S military is still prepared to go forward with a full, large scale assault on Iran if no deal is reached.

item.title: Taylor responds after Liberal senator breaks ranks over Coalition's migration policy
item.description: The Coalition should be wary of its tone and not approach migration as a "negative", a Liberal senator has said.

item.title: SBS News in Easy English 19 May 2026
item.description: A daily 5 minute news wrap for English learners and people with disability.

item.title: Families 'terrified' for Australians on Gaza flotilla detained by Israel
item.description: The wellbeing of 11 Australian activists is unknown after their Gaza-bound flotilla was intercepted overnight.

item.title: RBA warns of future business price hikes as ASX rebounds from seven week low
item.description: The Australian sharemarket bounced back from a seven-week low as easing tensions in the Middle East lifted investor confidence, with the ASX200 rising 1.2 per cent. Ricardo Gonçalves speaks with Westpac Senior Economist Matthew Hassan about why Australians remain deeply pessimistic despite a slight lift in consumer sentiment, as the Reserve Bank warns businesses are preparing further price hikes and workers push for higher wages. Plus, Michael Jenneke from UBS Global Wealth Management breaks down the day’s market action, including gains for supermarkets, banks and telcos, while energy stocks slipped as oil prices eased.

item.title: Middle Australia has been forgotten; that's why I support One Nation. My niece disagrees
item.description: Renee recently switched from voting for the Coalition to One Nation, while her niece Sarah plans on voting for a left-leaning party.

item.title: WHO 'deeply concerned about scale and speed' of deadly Ebola outbreak
item.description: The World Health Organization says 26 more suspected Ebola deaths have been recorded in 24 hours.

item.title: 'Our business partner, Anthony Albanese': The 47 per cent tax claim going viral after the budget
item.description: Startup founders and small business owners have spread AI-generated memes about proposed changes to the capital gains tax.

item.title: SBS News in Easy English 20 May 2026
item.description: A daily 5 minute news wrap for English learners and people with disability

item.title: 'Why shouldn't she talk to me?': Pauline Hanson reveals policy talks with Gina Rinehart
item.description: Pauline Hanson has also spoken to SBS News about running Muslim One Nation candidates, neo-Nazi supporters and western Sydney voters.

item.title: Second group of IS-linked women have no government support, Albanese says
item.description: The Opposition has criticised the government, saying they've not done enough to stop the IS-linked women from returning.

item.title: What proposed CGT changes mean for small business as ASX hits 7 week low
item.description: The ASX 200 has fallen to a fresh seven-week low, on fears of rising global interest rates. SBS Finance Editor Ricardo Gonçalves speaks with Francesco De Stradis from Ord Minnett about the market sell-off and the key drivers behind it. Plus, William Buck Tax Services Partner Todd Want breaks down the federal government’s proposed tax reforms after online memes claiming the Prime Minister would “own” 47 per cent of small businesses sparked confusion and debate.

item.title: 'My dear friend': Putin and Xi praise partnership during talks in Beijing
item.description: While Russia faces economic and financial sanctions from other countries, China remains a prominent and loyal customer.

item.title: Why it's been hard to detect and contain the world's broadening Ebola crisis
item.description: A rare Ebola strain is spreading through the conflict-hit Democratic Republic of the Congo's east.

item.title: Ex-Cuban president Raúl Castro indicted on murder charges as Trump escalates pressure
item.description: The 94-year-old has been indicted over the deadly 1996 downing of two civilian planes.

item.title: The visa rule change affecting Australians going to this popular travel destination
item.description: Aussies looking to escape the winter cold overseas could be in for a surprise.

item.title: Israel envoy says Ben-Gvir not reflective of country, as activist treatment condemned
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin ⁠Netanyahu said security minister Itamar Ben-Gvir's treatment of activists was wrong.

item.title: Israel envoy says Ben-Gvir not reflective of country, as activist treatment condemned
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin ⁠Netanyahu said security minister Itamar Ben-Gvir's treatment of activists was wrong.

item.title: Does an El Niño loom? Here's what winter could look like across Australia
item.description: Parts of Australia are seeing record rainfall, but the BoM says the drenching won't last.

item.title: INTERVIEW: SBS talks to One Nation leader Pauline Hanson
item.description: Pauline Hanson has spoken with SBS Chief Political Correspondent Anna Henderson in a wide-ranging interview following the federal government’s 2026/27 budget announcement.

item.title: SBS News in Easy English 21 May 2026
item.description: A daily five minute news wrap for English learners and people with disability. Listen to Australian and world news, and follow trending topics with SBS News Podcasts.

item.title: Coalition positions itself as small business champion after Labor's budget tax reforms
item.description: The Coalition has vowed to support the small business sector as consternation grows over tax changes affecting capital gains. In his budget reply, Shadow Treasurer Tim Wilson accused Labor of an attack on aspiration, and laid out the Liberal vision.

item.title: Australia endorses historic UN resolution for climate change accountability
item.description: Australia voted in favour of endorsing the advisory opinion despite being one of the world's leading exporters of coal and gas.

item.title: 'Great triangle reversed': Xi met Trump, then Putin. It showed the world could be shifting
item.description: One of the Chinese president's meetings centred on deals. The other focused on confrontation.

item.title: Why measles, diphtheria and whooping cough cases are rising in Australia
item.description: These diseases were considered rare in Australia, having no recorded outbreaks for decades — until now.

item.title: Israel's ambassador to Australia summoned over 'shocking' flotilla video
item.description: A Gaza-bound aid flotilla has not reached its destination. Instead, footage of activists aboard it being forced to kneel on the concrete at Israel’s Ashdod port, surrounded by armed officers and taunted by far-right minister Itamar Ben-Gvir, has triggered international outrage. Among those detained, according to organisers, were 11 Australians.

item.title: NSW’s first Migrant Workers Centre opens in Sydney
item.description: The New South Wales government has unveiled the state's first Migrant Workers Centre to support temporary visa holders. It means migrant workers in Sydney now have a dedicated hub to seek help over work exploitation. It’s backed by $6.5 million in state funding over the next four years to provide workplace, safety and immigration support to vulnerable migrants across the state.

item.title: New funding announced to fight deadly diphtheria outbreak in Australia
item.description: Australia's worst diphtheria outbreak in decades has spread across Northern Territory into Western Australia, Queensland and South Australia, with more than 230 [[as of May 21]] cases of the disease reported so far this year. Aboriginal Community Controlled Health Organisations and health workers are welcoming a $7 million federal government funding package aimed at supporting the response to the outbreak. But, experts say the re-emergence of the disease exposes serious gaps in the public health system.

item.title: Conservation protest takes aim at "needless" harvesting on Great Barrier Reef
item.description: Australia is home to the world's largest coral reef ecosystem, but conservationists say it's also the country's largest coral fishery. The sustainability of the Great Barrier Reef has been the subject of discussion among environmentalists for quite some time. Now, a group of conservationists have resorted to an unconventional method of putting the spotlight on the situation.

item.title: China’s revolving door of world leaders, DRC’s Ebola emergency & Trump’s ‘slush fund’
item.description: Beijing looks to capitalise on rival powers following back-to-back visits from the US and Russian presidents, as The Democratic Republic of the Congo struggles to contain a deadly Ebola outbreak. Plus, Donald Trump celebrates a landmark settlement with the IRS, and we investigate the art of the perfect diplomatic gift.

item.title: China’s revolving door of world leaders, DRC’s Ebola emergency & Trump’s ‘slush fund’
item.description: Beijing looks to capitalise on rival powers following back-to-back visits from the US and Russian presidents, as The Democratic Republic of the Congo struggles to contain a deadly Ebola outbreak. Plus, Donald Trump celebrates a landmark settlement with the IRS, and we investigate the art of the perfect diplomatic gift.

item.title: China’s revolving door of world leaders, DRC’s Ebola emergency & Trump’s ‘slush fund’
item.description: Beijing looks to capitalise on rival powers following back-to-back visits from the US and Russian presidents, as The Democratic Republic of the Congo struggles to contain a deadly Ebola outbreak. Plus, Donald Trump celebrates a landmark settlement with the IRS, and we investigate the art of the perfect diplomatic gift.

item.title: Jobless rate hits 4.5 year high as ASX posts strongest rally in weeks
item.description: Australia’s unemployment rate has climbed to its highest level since late 2021, with nearly 19,000 jobs lost and youth unemployment on the rise, raising fresh questions about the economic outlook and the path for interest rates. Deloitte Access Economics partner Stephen Smith breaks down what’s driving the shift and what it means in a climate of global uncertainty and elevated borrowing costs. Meanwhile, the sharemarket surged, with the ASX 200 posting its strongest gain in six weeks, lifted by materials, banks and real estate, as Heath Moss from HLM Investments unpacks what’s behind the rally and whether it can last.

item.title: Deported Australians on Gaza-bound flotilla allege abuse while detained by Israel
item.description: Israel's prison service has said all detainees were held "in accordance with the law, with full regard for their basic rights".

item.title: Deported Australians on Gaza-bound flotilla allege abuse while detained by Israel
item.description: Israel's prison service has said all detainees were held "in accordance with the law, with full regard for their basic rights".

item.title: 'They are very, very worried': Why Donald Trump's power could cut both ways
item.description: Trump-backed candidates are winning Republican primaries — but that could be a double-edged sword.

item.title: Second IS-linked group attempting return faces 'same consequences', Plibersek says
item.description: It's believed the cohort will travel to Australia from Damascus after leaving Syria's al-Roj detention camp.

item.title: The online threat linked to Australia's worst diphtheria outbreak in decades
item.description: The disease had almost vanished. Experts say its resurgence reflects deeper problems.

item.title: SBS News in Easy English 22 May 2026
item.description: A daily 5 minute news wrap for English learners and people with disability

item.title: Ebola case confirmed in rebel-held area of DR Congo
item.description: Health workers are racing to contain the Bundibugyo strain of the virus in the Democratic Republic of Congo, for which there is currently no approved vaccine or treatment. The World Health Organisation says the Ebola outbreak spreading through the eastern D-R-C into neighbouring Uganda could be far larger than officially reported. In cities like Goma and Bunia, families are still trying to continue daily life amid growing fears the outbreak is spreading beyond control.

item.title: Australia's 'death tax' debate: What we really pay when we die
item.description: Labor says their trust taxation reforms close trust loopholes. The Opposition says they tax inherited wealth by stealth.

item.title: 'Fake gas shortage': Premier's fracking comments draw fury from conservationists
item.description: The Conservation Council said WA was not facing a gas supply problem, but it did have a gas export problem.

item.title: Is space the next investment frontier? Elon Musk's SpaceX listing sparks investor buzz
item.description: The ASX finished the week higher as investors weighed strong gains in mining stocks against weakness in telcos, while attention also turned to the booming space economy. In this episode of SBS On the Money, Ricardo Gonçalves speaks with Pepperstone Head of Research Chris Weston about Elon Musk’s plans to list SpaceX at a reported valuation of US$1.75 trillion, what it means for future blockbuster IPOs including OpenAI and Anthropic, and whether space is becoming the next big investment opportunity. Chris Weston also breaks down the day on the Australian sharemarket and the outlook for interest rates.

item.title: The two best places to invest your money in Australia after 'major' budget shake-up
item.description: Significant changes to tax discounts in the 2026 federal budget are likely to shift how and where people choose to invest.

item.title: Australia's slowdown, family fortunes — and the investment bet heading sky-high
item.description: The key money stories this week, broken down simply — and what they mean for you.

item.title: Second-hand EV sales are surging - even classic cars are going electric
item.description: Australia’s electric vehicle (EV) market hit a new record in April. And sales of second-hand EVs are soaring too. Some owners are also converting classic cars.

item.title: INTERVIEW: SBS talks to Doctors Without Borders about the Ebola outbreak in the DRC
item.description: The World Health Organisation has sent a team of experts and supplies to the Democratic Republic of Congo to help combat the spread of Ebola. On Sunday, it declared the outbreak a public health emergency of international concern because of the high risk the disease could spread further beyond DR Congo's borders after two cases were confirmed in Kampala, the capital of neighbouring Uganda. Among the agencies assisting in the efforts is Medicins San Frontiers, also known as Doctors Without Borders. Simon Eccleshall is the Head of Programs for MSF in Australia, and he spoke with SBS World News reporter Alexandra Jones about the group's response.

item.title: INTERVIEW: SBS Nepali speaks to Bianca Adler after record breaking Mount Everest climb
item.description: A Melbourne teenager has made history, becoming the youngest Australian to reach the summit of Mount Everest.18-year-old Bianca Adler achieved the feat on her second attempt - completing the ascent of the nearly 9,000-metre [[8,849m]] summit with two Nepali guides. SBS Nepali executive producer Abhas Parajuli spoke to Bianca Adler about the challenges but also what it felt like being at the peak of the famous summit.

item.title: People with MS concerned about losing access to NDIS as survey reveals some already don't qualify for scheme
item.description: There are fears as the government seeks to achieve its budget savings with cutbacks to NDIS access, those who need support won't receive it. A new survey has found not all those with multiple sclerosis have access to the scheme - and for those who do, they face constant misunderstandings about their condition.

item.title: Inside the costly trend charging up Australia's classic car scene
item.description: A niche group of classic car owners are giving used vehicles an electric second life — but is it worth it?

item.title: Melbourne teenager becomes youngest Australian to summit Mount Everest
item.description: The 18-year-old's singular achievement comes as the number of climbers ascending to the world's tallest peak hits a record high.

item.title: SpaceX launches biggest model of Starship megarocket
item.description: SpaceX has launched its biggest Starship yet as the company races against Jeff Bezos' Blue Origin for space exploration dominance.

item.title: He made a formula to mock his profession. Then it correctly predicted three World Cups
item.description: The football fan has correctly predicted the World Cup winner since 2014. Here's the side he's picked as this year's champions.

item.title: A 'safe haven' in times of turmoil. Why demand for this country's 'golden visa' is rising
item.description: New Zealand has long been popular among backpackers and thrill-seekers. Now wealthy investors are flocking there too.

item.title: $10bn wipeout: Why investors are suddenly worried about an Australian giant
item.description: CSL's dramatic collapse has shattered investor confidence in one of Australia's most trusted companies.

item.title: The numbers don't tell the whole story about Australia's biggest migrant group
item.description: As migration dominates politics, Indian Australians say the social cost is becoming personal.

item.title: SBS sits down with independent MP Allegra Spender
item.description: SBS speaks with independent MP Allegra Spender to discuss the recent federal budget

item.title: INTERVIEW: May is Lupus Awareness Month. SBS has spoken to mental health advocate Jenny Lai about her journey
item.description: Every May, Lupus Awareness Month is recognised around the world, to highlight the chronic autoimmune disease which is still widely misunderstood. Lupus affects over five million people globally, and many of those affected are women. Jenny Lai is a mental health advocate whose Lupus diagnosis was a catalyst in her mental health journey. She has spoken to SBS' Alyssa Chandler.

item.title: Clarissa is beating rivals twice her age — but there's a 'real issue' behind the scenes
item.description: They're learning fast, winning early — and then leaving the chessboard behind.

item.title: How three Red Cross volunteers became the first victims of the latest Ebola outbreak
item.description: The Bundibugyo strain of Ebola was declared an emergency of international concern by the World Health Organization on Sunday.

item.title: Iranian football team moves FIFA World Cup base from United States to Mexico
item.description: Iran's participation in the World Cup has been in question since February, when the United States and Israel began bombing the country.

item.title: 'He thrives on controversy': The past and possible future of Israel's 'TikTok minister'
item.description: Itamar Ben-Gvir has risen to the highest level of Israeli politics, but he may not have a long future there.

item.title: They were lucky to survive — but one question still haunts them
item.description: For those left with guilt, trauma and grief, survival can be something some still struggle to make sense of.

item.title: 'Doesn't sound good': Fears for difficult citizenship choice under new Coalition policy
item.description: Opposition leader Angus Taylor has said that he would block non-citizens from access to 17 different welfare programs.

item.title: 'Tragic incident': Man dead after shark attack at popular Queensland diving spot
item.description: The 39-year-old was attacked by a shark while spear-fishing off Tully Heads with a second person.

item.title: Australians returning home from Gaza-bound aid flotilla allege abuse by Israeli authorities
item.description: Israel has denied the allegations, saying the detainees were "held in accordance with the law".

item.title: SBS News in Easy English 25 May 2026
item.description: A daily five minute news wrap for English learners and people with disability.

item.title: Teal independents in talks to set up their own party
item.description: Several so-called 'teal' independents are in advanced discussions about the potential formation of a new political party. Responding to growing distrust for the major parties and surging support for One Nation, some independent MPs say Australia needs a more sensible opposition.

item.title: 'A fighter': Tributes continue for AFL legend and motor neurone disease campaigner
item.description: A state memorial is being planned for Daniher, who has been remembered as "just incredibly selfless" and "a great family man".

item.title: China sends astronaut on year-long space mission as it races NASA to the moon
item.description: China has successfully docked its Shenzhou-23 spacecraft with the Tiangong space station, after a dramatic overnight launch from the edge of the Gobi Desert. The mission carries three astronauts into orbit, including the first astronaut from Hong Kong, and one crew member expected to remain in space for a full year, double the usual duration of previous Chinese missions.

item.title: Why oil prices will remain high even if the war ends, and why stamp prices to rise
item.description: Oil prices have tumbled after Donald Trump suggested a peace deal between the US and Iran could be near, raising hopes of lower fuel costs for Australian motorists. SBS On the Money speaks with MST Financial’s Saul Kavonic about why petrol prices may ease in the short term, but why oil markets are unlikely to return to pre-conflict levels anytime soon.

item.title: Iran confirms progress in US talks, but insists no agreement imminent
item.description: Iran's foreign ministry spokesman Esmaeil Baqaei accused the United States of "contradictions" and shifting its positions.

item.title: The hidden costs delaying once-in-lifetime pilgrimage for these Australians
item.description: For some Australian Muslims, the dream of Hajj is being tested by rising costs and new challenges.

item.title: Tributes flow for AFL legend Neale Daniher | Morning News Bulletin 26 May 2026
item.description: Tributes flow for AFL great and campaigner Neale Daniher; Two killed in an Israeli air strike on the southern Gaza Strip; And in sport, Italian cyclist Enrico Zanoncello expelled from the Giro d’Italia for headbutting a rival.

item.title: INTERVIEW: Stolen Generations survivor on significance of Sorry Day and ongoing fight for redress
item.description: The impacts of the Stolen Generation policies on families were detailed publicly for the first time in the landmark 'Bringing Them Home' report 29 years ago, following a national inquiry that featured the testimonies of more than 500 Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander peoples. Many recommendations from the report on redress and support for survivors still remain unimplemented. The Healing Foundation's David Wragge has spoken with Biwa Kwan about marking Sorry Day, and the work that remains to ensure appropriate redress and support is provided to ageing survivors. First Nations listeners seeking support can contact 13YARN on 13 92 76.

item.title: Russia attacks Kyiv, threatening the start of a 'series of systematic strikes'
item.description: Russia says the strikes were in response to the attack on student accommodation in the Russian-controlled Luhansk region.

item.title: Israeli strike kills six-year-old girl and a woman in Gaza, medics say
item.description: Health authorities in the Gaza Strip say 900 people have died in attacks since a truce started in October.

item.title: Iranian envoy in Qatar for peace talks as both US and Iran play down imminent breakthrough
item.description: US secretary of state Marco Rubio says the US will prioritise diplomacy before considering the deal in "another way".

item.title: Play on: Fight against motor neurone disease continues after Neale Daniher loses battle with 'The Beast'
item.description: Former Melbourne captain Garry Lyon has called on Prime Minister Anthony Albanese to write a "dirty big cheque" for FightMND in honour of Neale Daniher. The AFL great died at the age of 65 after fighting Motor Neurone Disease for 13 years.

item.title: The 'anomaly' that derailed Vivid Sydney's biggest-ever drone show
item.description: Here's where the "biggest drone show program in Vivid Sydney's history" went wrong

item.title: Energy prices set to fall in some eastern Australia states | Midday News Bulletin 26 May 2026
item.description: Relief for households with energy prices set to fall; Women linked to the IS group expected to face charges when they arrive back in Australia this evening; And in sport, a key Socceroos player out of the World Cup.

item.title: Suspected spray attack in Tokyo shopping centre leaves two dozen in hospital
item.description: Authorities said that a man allegedly sprayed a substance inside the building.

item.title: Energy bill relief: Here's how much power prices are set to fall
item.description: Residents and small businesses across four states could see their energy bills reduced in the new financial year.

item.title: SBS News in Easy English 26 May 2026
item.description: A daily five minute news wrap for English learners and people with disability.

item.title: Man accused of throwing bomb at Perth Invasion Day rally makes first court appearance
item.description: Of the three indictments the accused man faces, engaging in a terrorist act is the first such charge in the state's history.

item.title: New Zealand property may be tempting Australians — but there's a major catch
item.description: While New Zealand's lack of a capital gains tax is turning heads after the federal budget, experts are urging caution.

item.title: Sorry won't cut it anymore: calls grow for action on Bringing Them Home recommendations
item.description: Almost three decades on from the landmark Bringing Them Home report on Stolen Generations, the government has implemented just six per cent of recommendations. This National Sorry Day, advocates are calling for government action to better support survivors and their families.

item.title: US launches new strikes on Iranian targets | Evening News Bulletin 26 May 2026
item.description: In this bulletin, the US launches fresh strikes on Iranian targets near the Strait of Hormuz, two men arrested in the investigation into fugitive Dezi Freeman. And in football, 29 Socceroos players fight for a place on the World Cup squad.

item.title: IS-linked families return to Australia from Syria: Here's what we know about them
item.description: Seven women with links to the self-proclaimed IS-group, and their children, have arrived weeks after three women were arrested.

item.title: Ebola numbers continue to rise
item.description: Cases of Ebola continue to rise in the eastern part of the Democratic Republic of Congo, with the number of suspected cases approaching 1,000, and suspected deaths at more than 200. Aid groups are warning they need more funding to tackle the disease, and health workers and volunteers are working to educate people and quash misinformation.

item.title: What are the Abraham Accords - and why do they matter?
item.description: US President Donald Trump has called on a range of Gulf countries to improve their relationship with Israel as negotiations continue with Iran on an agreement to end the war. In a post on Truth Social, Trump has called on more Arab and Muslim states to sign up to the Abraham Accords, which were brokered during his first term in office. Here, we explain what the accords are, and why they're so significant.

item.title: Why New Zealand property is suddenly drawing attention in Australia
item.description: As Australians furiously debate changes to housing taxes, discussion has emerged about whether investors could look to New Zealand for more favourable tax treatments. That's because New Zealand does not impose a broad capital gains tax on investment properties like Australia does. But experts warn there's a catch.

item.title: Space economy hype grows, as businesses pull back on investments because of fuel costs
item.description: Australian businesses are feeling the pressure from rising fuel costs and supply issues, with new ABS data showing nearly three quarters have been negatively impacted. Business NSW Executive Director David Borger joins Ricardo Gonçalves on the SBS On the Money Podcast to discuss how companies are responding and what it means for prices and inflation ahead of key data tomorrow.

item.title: 'Still going in 2028': The issue that may stall US-Iran peace efforts for years
item.description: Trump has said that a peace deal with Iran is close but what exactly are the two countries in disagreement over?

item.title: Iranian supreme leader: Gulf region will 'no longer serve as shields' for US bases
item.description: Ayatollah Mojtaba Khamenei issued the statement after the US confirmed missile strikes on Iranian missile sites and boats.

item.title: Four dead after train collides with school bus in Belgium
item.description: A police spokesperson told the media that seven children, a supervisor and a driver were aboard the minibus.

item.title: 'Not what it used to be': Is the weather too hot for Euro summer travel?
item.description: Europe's heatwave is a sign of things to come. Will Australian tourists be deterred?

item.title: I was lost in the bush for 10 days with my son. A note left on a bench saved our lives
item.description: In 2017, Michelle Pittman and her son set out on a bushwalk, not realising that one wrong turn would lead to a fight for survival.

item.title: More women linked to IS group fighters return to Australia | Morning News Bulletin 27 May 2026
item.description: Women and children linked to the IS group return to Australia; Four killed in a collision between a school bus and train in Belgium; And in sport, Mitch Moses ruled out of the State of Origin opener in Sydney.

item.title: Iran says US attacks violate ceasefire, as Israel pounds Lebanon with strikes
item.description: Iran has accused the US of breaching their ceasefire and warns it's ready to retaliate, while Israel is intensifying its attacks on Lebanon.

item.title: Autism: it's not just a male thing anymore
item.description: New research shows that the ratio between girls and boys with autism is much closer than once thought. It's understood that the current ratio of males to females with autism is 4 to 1, however, new studies suggests it could be as low as 2 to 1. While this gap is slowly closing, women over the age of forty are becoming one of the key groups to seek diagnosis.

item.title: Australia's major employment services shake-up: Here's what's changing — and what's not
item.description: The $312 million overhaul maintains one element critics say is "punishment as usual".

item.title: Australia's major employment services shake-up: Here's what's changing — and what's not
item.description: The $312 million overhaul maintains one element critics say is "punishment as usual".

item.title: Parents call for UK to implement tougher social media rules for underage children
item.description: A warning - the following story contains elements that may distress some people. The UK could become the latest country to bring in major restrictions on children’s use of social media. Campaigners say the platforms can be so damaging to under 16s that some have harmed themselves or even taken their lives.

item.title: 'Not far away': Rescue team confident of reaching group trapped for days in Laos cave
item.description: The team involved in the 2018 Thai cave rescue is part of the Laotian efforts.

item.title: Labor flags employment services overhaul after litany of complaints | Midday News Bulletin 27 May 2026
item.description: Major changes announced to employment services for jobless Australians; Westpac fined $26 million over financial hardship cases; And in sport, more than $12 million announced to fund this year's Rugby League World Cup.

item.title: Inflation falls as fuel prices ease — but rate rise fears remain
item.description: Headline inflation fell in April thanks to a cut in the fuel excise, but the underlying impulse is still gathering steam.

item.title: 'I know you are busy': Students apologetic, 'short-changed' as university class sizes explode
item.description: Universities have been accused of compromising Australian education, with a major report showing class sizes have skyrocketed since COVID-19.

item.title: Australian Muslims lament rising cost of Hajj as pilgrimage unfolds in Mecca
item.description: As millions of Muslims wake to messages of “Eid Mubarak” this morning, more than one-and-a-half million pilgrims are completing the rituals of Hajj in Saudi Arabia. For those who make it, the pilgrimage is often described as the journey of a lifetime, a moment of forgiveness, surrender and renewal. But for many Australian Muslims, the fifth pillar of Islam is becoming harder to reach, because the cost has become overwhelming.

item.title: SBS News in Easy English 27 May 2026
item.description: A daily five minute news wrap for English learners and people with disability.

item.title: Renewed calls for Indigenous Voice to Parliament as Reconciliation Week begins
item.description: Recent Closing the Gap reports show the need for a voice had not diminished, campaigners say.

item.title: Debate begins over proposed employment services changes | Evening News Bulletin 27 May 2026
item.description: The federal government launches its major shake-up of Australia’s employment services system... Ali France referred to the Australian Electoral Commission... and Nishan Velupillay feeling good about his form, with the Socceroos' World Cup squad announcement just days away

item.title: As the UK faces unprecedented heatwaves, how did the world's biggest cities cope in the past?
item.description: From fire hydrants to rooftops, cities around the world have developed very different methods for staying cool in a heatwave.

item.title: Businesses pass on fuel costs despite easing headline inflation
item.description: Australia’s sharemarket rebounded after a softer-than-expected inflation report lifted hopes the RBA may hold interest rates in June, but ABS data showed some businesses are passing on higher fuel costs to consumers. SBS On the Money explores what the latest CPI figures mean for households, the Reserve Bank and investors, with analysis from Sunny Nguyen, Head of Australia Economics at Moody’s Analytics, and Niv Dagan from Peak Asset Management.

item.title: Eating from bins, watered down food: Desperate ways Aussies are trying to make ends meet
item.description: Food charities say there's an emerging group of Australians seeking food relief for the first time.

item.title: Hamas confirms Israeli strikes killed its new military leader in Gaza
item.description: Thousands of people gathered in Gaza City for the joint funeral of Mohammed Odeh and members of his family.

item.title: Hamas confirms Israeli strikes killed its new military leader in Gaza
item.description: Thousands of people gathered in Gaza City for the joint funeral of Mohammed Odeh and members of his family.

item.title: 'Shameful': Outrage as New Zealand scraps its standalone environment department
item.description: Legal changes will now see the environment managed under a "mega-ministry" covering housing, urban development, and transport.

item.title: Newly elected Solomon Islands prime minister set to visit Australia in first official trip
item.description: Matthew Wale was elected prime minister in May following a no-confidence vote against his predecessor.

item.title: IS-linked woman attempted to board flight to Australia despite travel ban
item.description: The woman had been issued a temporary exclusion order and was stopped at the airport in Damascus.

item.title: Five people trapped in Laos cave found alive, but rescuers say extraction will be complex
item.description: Rescue teams are still searching for the two remaining people from the group.

item.title: Weeks in the dark, breathing helium: The Australians doing one of the world's weirdest jobs
item.description: Saturation diving could be the world’s most intriguing, dangerous and isolating job. Reg and Chris explain why they think it’s also the best.

item.title: 'Nobody really knows': Australia's property market has taken on a strange new mood
item.description: Homebuyers are navigating a market unlike anything seen in a very long time, an expert says.

item.title: Trump waiting for ‘perfect deal’ in Iran peace talks | Morning News Bulletin 28 May 2026
item.description: Donald Trump suggests an Iran deal may depend on Arab nations signing the Abraham Accords; housing tax changes to be introduced to parliament today; and the Blues take a win at State of Origin game one.

item.title: US and Iran trade strikes as peace deal stalemate drags; Israel targets Lebanon's south
item.description: Israel's military ordered all residents in southern Lebanon to evacuate, signalling further escalation despite a ceasefire.

item.title: US and Iran trade strikes as peace deal stalemate drags; Israel targets Lebanon's south
item.description: Israel's military ordered all residents in southern Lebanon to evacuate, signalling further escalation despite a ceasefire.

item.title: Reform or rebrand? Advocates say employment system is still unfit for work
item.description: The government has announced what it has described as the biggest reform to employment services in over 30 years. The system has been set up to help unemployed Australians find work. But with the continuation of mutual obligations and private providers, advocates say the changes are nothing more than a rebrand.

item.title: Seven dead, officials on alert as Europe swelters through spring that feels like summer
item.description: Authorities in France say at least seven people have died due to unseasonably warm temperatures, while for the second day in a row, the UK has recorded a new record high for May. Much of Western Europe is still dealing with a pre-summer heatwave, leading to health warnings across the continent.

item.title: Will Australia follow the US and Canada on Ebola travel restrictions?
item.description: The Department of Health says it is "closely monitoring" the situation.

item.title: The rare blue 'micromoon' peaks this weekend. Here's when you should look up
item.description: It's called a blue moon — but this month's full moon comes with a rare twist.

item.title: New plan to get up to $3,000 back in pockets of scam-hit Australians
item.description: Critics have said such a move could embolden scammers but the government insists its proposal is a "balanced approach".

item.title: Government launches record breaking PFAS contamination lawsuit | Midday News Bulletin 28 May 2026
item.description: The Federal Government sues a manufacturer over alleged chemical contamination at 28 defence sites, a woman who returned to Australia from Syria charged by terrorism police and Novak Djokovic moves to the third round of the French Open.

item.title: Federal government sues 3M over PFAS contamination
item.description: The Australian government is suing multinational industrial company 3M in the largest ever claim brought by the Commonwealth. The legal action will seek damages in relation to PFAS contamination across defence sites throughout Australia, alleging the company withheld information about their products.

item.title: IS-linked woman who returned to Australia last year charged with terrorism offences
item.description: The woman has been charged with entering and remaining in a declared conflict zone and being a member of a terrorist organisation.

item.title: Detainees at US immigration centre on hunger strike amid concern over conditions, high suicide rates
item.description: Hundreds of people in an immigration detention centre in the US state of New Jersey are on a hunger strike, according to their families. They're protesting poor conditions inside the Delaney Hall facility. It comes as an investigation found 10 people have died by suicide in immigration detention since President Donald Trump took office at the beginning of last year and launched an aggressive deportation strategy.

item.title: Labor moves to push through controversial tax reforms despite backlash
item.description: Treasurer Jim Chalmers introduced what he called a "bill for workers, first home buyers and future generations" to parliament.

item.title: 3M to 'defend ourselves' in historic $2 billion legal challenge over 'forever chemicals'
item.description: The Australian government says the US manufacturer withheld information about its toxic products that have caused health and land damage.

item.title: SBS News in Easy English 28 May 2026
item.description: A daily five-minute news wrap for English learners and people with disability.

item.title: Australians from hantavirus-hit cruise issued extended quarantine orders
item.description: The passengers have tested negative, but authorities say the risk of transmission after disembarkation has not passed.

item.title: 3M vows to defend itself against the federal government’s PFAS lawsuit | Evening News Bulletin 28 May 2026
item.description: 3M vows to defend itself against the federal government’s PFAS lawsuit; More than 250 diphtheria cases recorded across Australia’s north; And in cricket, Teen sensation Vaibhav Sooryavanshi smashes an IPL sixes record with 97 off 29 balls.

item.title: Iran-US peace talks stall, Türkiye targets dissent & Trump’s excellent excuse
item.description: Conflicting reports on peace negotiations between the US and Iran as military tensions escalate across the region. Former political prisoner Dr Kylie Moore-Gilbert joins us to explore what it means for the Iranian regime and its citizens. Plus, Netanyahu vows to crush Hezbollah with an expanding offensive as his own leadership comes under pressure ahead of elections. While in Türkiye, Erdogan moves against the opposition in a fresh crackdown on dissent, and Trump’s patriotic excuse for dodging a family function.

item.title: Fire in Kenya girls' school dormitory kills 16, injures 73
item.description: Local authorities said the fire broke out in a dormitory where 220 girls had been sleeping.

item.title: Mapped: The 'forever chemical' sites at the centre of Australia's $2bn legal fight
item.description: A total of 28 sites across Australia are in the middle of the government's largest legal claim ever.

item.title: Mount Everest climber smashes 23-year record for fastest ever summit
item.description: American climber Tyler Andrews broke the previous, long-standing record by just over an hour.

item.title: Mount Everest climber smashes 23-year record for fastest ever summit
item.description: American climber Tyler Andrews broke the previous, long-standing record by just over an hour.

item.title: Israel strikes southern Beirut for the second time since ceasefire took hold
item.description: The escalation comes as Lebanon and Israel prepare for talks between military delegations in the US.

item.title: Israel strikes southern Beirut for the second time since ceasefire took hold
item.description: The escalation comes as Lebanon and Israel prepare for talks between military delegations in the US.

item.title: Trading deadly attacks with Hezbollah again, Israel warns of 'great force'. Why now?
item.description: Israel has started its biggest attacks on southern Lebanon since the ceasefire started — the timing is no coincidence.

item.title: Is 'baby brain' real? Nearly 300 parents put the theory to the test
item.description: Many new parents describe the mental fog of early parenthood as 'baby brain'. But Australian researchers found a surprise.

item.title: US, Iran agree to extend ceasefire | Morning News Bulletin 29 May 2026
item.description: Donald Trump yet to approve a US Iran ceasefire extension; quarantine extended for Australians caught up in cruise ship hantavirus outbreak; the date is named for a state memorial to honour AFL legend Neale Daniher.

item.title: 'For us, by us': Indigenous plea for truth-telling and connection during Reconciliation Week
item.description: Community led events are being held across the country to mark National Reconciliation Week, which runs from May 27 to Jun 03. This year's theme is 'All In' - a call for all Australians to commit wholeheartedly to reconciliation, and to advancing Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander people's rights.

item.title: Man sentenced for plotting attack on Taylor Swift Vienna concert
item.description: In his testimony last month, the 21-year-old said he had become convinced he "had to wage jihad".

item.title: Iran and US reportedly reach tentative deal to extend ceasefire
item.description: The deal, which is still pending sign-off, would allow traffic to flow through the Strait of Hormuz, according to a US official.

item.title: 'Like diving in coffee': The toughest challenges facing the Laos cave rescue
item.description: An Australian diver is joining the mission — and says he's afraid of what's ahead.

item.title: Benjamin Netanyahu says Israel will expand control of Gaza to 70 per cent
item.description: The Israeli prime minister said increasing control of Palestinian territory will "press" Hamas from "all sides".

item.title: UN condemns latest Israeli strike on Lebanon | Midday News Bulletin 29 May 2026
item.description: UN condemns the latest Israeli strikes in Lebanon; Labor criticises the Liberal party as Tony Abbott is elected federal party president; Socceroos heading to Los Angeles ahead of opening World Cup game.

item.title: 'Very rough day': Jeff Bezos' Blue Origin rocket explodes on launch pad
item.description: The New Glenn rocket experienced an "anomaly", the company said, exploding during a test.

item.title: That’s not a heatwave, THIS is a heatwave, says WMO
item.description: A new World Meteorological Organisation report has found the odds are high that the next five years will smash global heat records.The new United Nations climate projections forecast a high likelihood of more speeded-up warming through 2030, with Earth smashing its record for the hottest year and regularly surging past the international climate limit set as safe in 2015.

item.title: SBS News in Easy English 29 May 2026
item.description: A daily 5 minute news wrap for English learners and people with disability.

item.title: 'What a surprise': Butler hits back at forecast suggesting tax reforms will hurt renters
item.description: Labor minister Mark Butler put the discrepancies between the forecasts down to the "vested interests" of property groups.

item.title: How a major shock reshaped Australia and the key question we now face
item.description: How did the fuel price surge reshape daily life? More than 2,000 Australians offer some clues.

item.title: US Vice President says ceasefire deal close with Tehran | Evening News Bulletin 29 May 2026
item.description: US Vice President JD Vance says Washington and Tehran are close to a ceasefire deal; two people arrested as police clear protesters from Brisbane's Victoria Park; and in sport, Serena Williams could be poised for a return to tennis after nearly four years away from the game.

item.title: Temporary migrants struggling due to pandemic
item.description: New revelations highlight the extent of hardship being felt by temporary migrants in Australia, sparking a renewed push for the Federal Government support.

item.title: ASX soars on US-Iran ceasefire hopes, Australia’s richest people
item.description: Following Thursday’s $45 billion wipeout, the S&P/ASX 200 has finished the week 1.62 per cent higher following reports that US and Iranian negotiators have reached a tentative deal to extend the ceasefire by 60 days. As a result, oil prices fell sharply while gold and silver rose. For more, Stephanie Youssef spoke with Opal Capital Management Chief Investment Officer Omkar Joshi. Also, the combined wealth of Australia's 200 richest people has surged to more than $700 billion, with mining magnate Gina Rinehart once again leading the Australian Financial Review’s 2026 Rich List. For more, Stephanie Youssef spoke with AFR’s Rich List Deputy Editor Hannah Tattersall.
